rationale: The cited paper is introduced in the opening paragraph as the motivating "tour de force" and is revisited in the conclusion as making "important progress," indicating a central framing role. Beyond a passing mention, Camerer draws on specific elements from Novemsky and Kahneman (2005), including the benchmark loss-aversion coefficient and the role of budget constraints, to support and interpret his own arguments. The citation thus meaningfully structures the essay's conceptual setup and interpretation rather than serving as generic background.
relation_description: Camerer's note is explicitly anchored to Novemsky and Kahneman (2005) as a key basis for discussing when and how loss aversion should be used. He builds his broader theoretical commentary around their boundary findings and uses them as an empirical and conceptual reference point.
